text: Patient reports missed period followed by three positive home pregnancy tests
transcript_quote: My period did not come when it was supposed to, and I am usually pretty regular... Both had the two lines.

text: Normal pregnancy confirmed
transcript_quote: Everything today says normal, healthy pregnancy.

text: Patient reports fatigue that sneaks up on her
transcript_quote: Tired in a way that sneaks up on me — like, sit down on the couch at seven and wake up at nine tired.

text: Patient reports morning nausea when she waits too long to eat, relieved by crackers before rising
transcript_quote: A little queasy in the mornings if I wait too long to eat. Crackers before I even get up seem to fix it.

text: Eating crackers before getting up relieves morning nausea
transcript_quote: Crackers before I even get up seem to fix it.

text: Denies uncontrolled vomiting, dizziness, or fainting
transcript_quote: No, nothing like that. Just the wooziness if I am hungry.

text: Reports mild wooziness only when hungry
transcript_quote: Just the wooziness if I am hungry.

text: Denies vaginal bleeding or spotting
transcript_quote: No bleeding, no.

text: Reports occasional mild low pulling/stretching sensation, no pain or cramping
transcript_quote: Maybe the tiniest pulling feeling low down once in a while, like a stretch, but not pain.

text: Reports increased urinary frequency but denies burning with urination and fevers
transcript_quote: Going more often, yes, but I read that is just pregnancy. No burning.

text: History of recurrent urinary tract infections, at least a couple per year since her twenties

slots.frequency: a couple per year since twenties

transcript_quote: I get urinary tract infections all the time. Like, at least a couple every year since my twenties.

text: Urine culture obtained today and to be repeated routinely at visits given recurrent UTI history

slots.reason: recurrent UTI history, asymptomatic bacteriuria screening

transcript_quote: today we send a urine culture no matter what, because in pregnancy an infection can simmer without symptoms

text: Counseled that any positive culture will be treated promptly with pregnancy-safe medication
transcript_quote: If anything grows, we treat it promptly with medicine that is safe in pregnancy.

text: Stopped birth control when trying to conceive; currently takes no regular medications, only occasional OTC for headache
transcript_quote: I stopped my birth control a while back when we decided to start trying, and since then it is just the occasional thing for a headache. Nothing regular.

text: Counseled to check with provider before taking any new medication including OTC
transcript_quote: before you take anything new, even over the counter, run it past us.

text: In-office urine pregnancy test performed, positive/confirmed pregnancy
transcript_quote: the office urine test agrees with your three home tests. You are officially pregnant.

text: Physical examination performed, normal
transcript_quote: Exam first: everything looks healthy and normal.

text: Fundal height evaluated; uterus size appropriate for early gestation
transcript_quote: Your uterus feels the size I would expect this early — I checked the fundal height

text: Ultrasound confirmed intrauterine, viable pregnancy with fetal heartbeat

slots.result: intrauterine viable pregnancy, cardiac activity present

transcript_quote: A viable pregnancy, right where it should be.

text: Fetal heart auscultation performed, normal heart sounds
transcript_quote: I also listened in — the heart sounds are exactly what we want to hear.

text: Blood group typing ordered/performed
transcript_quote: your blood type

text: Complete blood count/hemogram ordered/performed to check for anemia
transcript_quote: a full blood count to check for anemia

text: Hepatitis B surface antigen test performed
transcript_quote: hepatitis B

text: Hepatitis C antibody test performed
transcript_quote: hepatitis C

text: HIV test performed
transcript_quote: HIV

text: Syphilis screening test performed
transcript_quote: and syphilis

text: Rubella immunity screening performed
transcript_quote: checking your immunity to rubella

text: Varicella (chickenpox) immunity screening performed
transcript_quote: and chickenpox

text: Urine chlamydia test performed
transcript_quote: We also test urine for chlamydia and gonorrhea

text: Urine gonorrhea test performed
transcript_quote: We also test urine for chlamydia and gonorrhea

text: Urine screened for glucose/sugar
transcript_quote: screen your urine for sugar and protein

text: Urine screened for protein
transcript_quote: screen your urine for sugar and protein

text: Pap smear performed as patient was due
transcript_quote: do a Pap smear if you are due — and you are due

text: Tuberculosis skin (Tine) test placed on forearm, to be read in 2-3 days
transcript_quote: We will also place a little tuberculosis skin test on your forearm today; someone just has to look at it in two to three days

text: Patient reports history of fainting with blood draws; accommodation planned (lying down with juice)
transcript_quote: I am a fainter. Historically.

text: Counseled that one regular cup of coffee per day is acceptable
transcript_quote: A regular cup a day is fine.

text: Counseled to avoid alcohol, smoking, and recreational drugs
transcript_quote: the true no-go list is alcohol, smoking, and anything recreational.

text: Patient reports never smoking and no alcohol since positive pregnancy test
transcript_quote: None of those are in the picture. Never smoked, and I have not had a drink since the two lines.

text: Counseled on UTI red-flag symptoms warranting same-day call: burning, urgency beyond usual, one-sided back pain, fever
transcript_quote: Burning, urgency beyond the usual, back pain on one side, fever — those are call-us-today symptoms, not wait-and-see symptoms.

text: Prenatal visits scheduled every four weeks for now, increasing later in pregnancy
transcript_quote: We will see her every four weeks for now, more often later in the pregnancy.

text: Nurse visit to be booked this week to read TB skin test
transcript_quote: book two things: the quick visit to read the test this week

text: Lab panel results to be posted to patient portal with call for anything requiring action
transcript_quote: Results from today's panel come to your patient portal, and we call about anything that needs action.

text: Counseled to continue normal activity, work, walks, and normal diet
transcript_quote: Live your life. Eat well, keep the crackers strategy, sleep when the couch claims you

text: Counseled that there is no medical rule on when to announce the pregnancy
transcript_quote: No medical rule — that one is entirely yours to decide, together.

text: Patient attended visit with husband, who is supportive and engaged
transcript_quote: this is my husband, Marcus. I told him he is contractually obligated to be at the first one.
